title: Peucedanum lowei
intro: Peucedanum lowei, also known as Low's hog's fennel or Low's cicely, is a perennial plant in the carrot family (Apiaceae) native to the western coast of North America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments, including digestive issues and skin conditions. Peucedanum lowei contains a range of bioactive constituents, including sesquiterpenes, coumarins, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms associated with various health conditions.
taxonomy: Peucedanum lowei is classified as a species of flowering plant in the carrot family Apiaceae. It is a member of the genus Peucedanum, which comprises several species of plants native to various parts of the world. The plant is typically found in mountainous regions and is characterized by its distinctive leaves and umbels of small flowers. As a member of the Apiaceae family, Peucedanum lowei is closely related to other plants such as carrots and parsley. Further classification may be provided at the subspecies or cultivar level, depending on the specific characteristics of the plant.
